resettlement.title: Resettlement & Durable Solutions
resettlement.summary: We help displaced families secure land, shelter, and a lasting place to rebuild. (DEMO)
resettlement.problem: Many displaced families in Hiran and Southwest State lack secure land tenure and safe shelter, leaving them without a stable place to rebuild. (DEMO)
resettlement.whatWeDo: We support site selection, land tenure documentation, shelter construction, and integration support with host communities. (DEMO)
resettlement.whereText: Active in Hiran/Hirshabelle (Beledweyne) and Southwest State (Baidoa). (DEMO)
resettlement.measurement: We track households resettled, land tenure documents issued, and household satisfaction surveys. (DEMO)
resettlement.story: Halima and her three children were displaced twice before resettling near Beledweyne with support securing land and building a permanent shelter. Name and details changed to protect identity. (DEMO STORY)
resettlement.storyAttribution: Shared with consent for illustrative purposes; not a real client account. (DEMO STORY)

shelter.title: Shelter & Essential Services
shelter.summary: We provide shelter kits and connect resettled families to water, health, and education. (DEMO)
shelter.problem: Resettled families often lack access to safe shelter and basic services like clean water, health care, and education. (DEMO)
shelter.whatWeDo: We distribute shelter kits and connect resettled households to water, health, and education services. (DEMO)
shelter.whereText: Active in Hiran/Hirshabelle and Southwest State, alongside our resettlement sites. (DEMO)
shelter.measurement: We track shelter kits distributed and households connected to essential services. (DEMO)
shelter.story: After resettling in Baidoa, Ahmed's family received a shelter kit and was connected to a nearby health clinic. Name and details changed to protect identity. (DEMO STORY)
shelter.storyAttribution: Shared with consent for illustrative purposes; not a real client account. (DEMO STORY)

livelihoods.title: Livelihoods & Self-Reliance
livelihoods.summary: We offer skills training and small business support so families move beyond aid dependency. (DEMO)
livelihoods.problem: Without stable income, displaced families remain dependent on aid long after resettlement. (DEMO)
livelihoods.whatWeDo: We provide skills training, cash-for-work opportunities, and small business grants. (DEMO)
livelihoods.whereText: Active in Hiran/Hirshabelle and Southwest State. (DEMO)
livelihoods.measurement: We track trainings completed, grants disbursed, and household income over time. (DEMO)
livelihoods.story: With a small business grant, Fartun opened a tailoring shop in Beledweyne, now employing two other resettled women. Name and details changed to protect identity. (DEMO STORY)
livelihoods.storyAttribution: Shared with consent for illustrative purposes; not a real client account. (DEMO STORY)

protection.title: Protection & Community Cohesion
protection.summary: We run safeguarding, referral, and dispute-resolution work between displaced and host communities. (DEMO)
protection.problem: Displacement can increase risks of gender-based violence, child protection concerns, and tension between displaced and host communities. (DEMO)
protection.whatWeDo: We run safeguarding services, GBV referral pathways, child protection support, and community dispute resolution. (DEMO)
protection.whereText: Active in Hiran/Hirshabelle and Southwest State. (DEMO)
protection.measurement: We track referrals made, disputes resolved, and community feedback sessions held. (DEMO)
protection.story: A community dispute-resolution session in Baidoa helped a displaced family and host neighbors agree on shared water access. Details changed to protect identity. (DEMO STORY)
protection.storyAttribution: Shared with consent for illustrative purposes; not a real client account. (DEMO STORY)

impact.complaintsIntro: If you have a concern about our programs or the conduct of our staff, you can reach us through any of the channels below. Reports can be made anonymously and will never affect your or your family's access to assistance.
impact.reportsEmptyState: Our first annual report will be published here in [YEAR TO BE CONFIRMED].

news.consentNotice: Names in personal stories may be changed to protect identity, and are shared only with the individual's informed consent.

about.heading: About Us
about.whoWeAreBody: Horumar Resettlement Network is a Somali-led organization supporting families displaced by conflict and climate shocks as they resettle and rebuild in Hiran and Southwest State. (DEMO)
about.missionBody: To help displaced families secure safe shelter, sustainable livelihoods, and a lasting place to belong. (DEMO)
about.visionBody: A Somalia where no family remains displaced without a path to a stable, dignified life. (DEMO)
about.ourStoryBody: Founded in response to displacement across Hiran and Southwest State, our organization grew from local relief efforts into a structured resettlement and livelihoods program. (DEMO)

whereWeWork.heading: Where We Work
whereWeWork.intro: We work in two areas of Somalia: Hiran/Hirshabelle and Southwest State. (DEMO)

hiran.heading: Hiran / Hirshabelle
hiran.displacementContext: In May 2023, the Shabelle River burst its banks at Beledweyne in its worst flooding in roughly 30 years, affecting an estimated 200,000+ people and flooding around 79% of the town — one of several conflict- and climate-driven displacement waves in the region. (Sources: IOM, OCHA)

southwest.heading: Southwest State
southwest.displacementContext: Southwest State has experienced repeated displacement driven by conflict and recurring drought across South-Central Somalia. (general, well-established context; specific incident data pending)

privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains what information Horumar Resettlement Network collects through this website, how it is used, and what is never collected.
privacy.whatWeCollectBody: Today, the only personal information this website collects is what you choose to submit through the contact form: your name, email address, subject, and message. We do not use cookies, and no analytics or tracking software is active on this site.
privacy.thirdPartyBody: Contact form submissions are processed by Formspree, a third-party form service, solely to deliver your message to our staff. We do not share your information with any other third party.
privacy.whatWeNeverCollectBody: We never collect the names, locations, family details, or biometric data of people we assist, and this website has no feature to register for or apply for assistance.
privacy.retentionBody: Contact form messages are retained only as long as needed to respond to your inquiry, and are not used for any other purpose.
privacy.contactBody: If you have questions about this policy or wish to request that we delete information you have submitted, contact us using the details on our Contact page.
privacy.changesBody: We may update this policy as our practices change. The date of the most recent update will be noted here once this policy is finalized.

safeguarding.heading: Safeguarding & PSEA Policy
safeguarding.intro: Horumar Resettlement Network is committed to protecting the people we serve, our staff, and our partners from harm, exploitation, and abuse.
safeguarding.zeroToleranceBody: We maintain a zero-tolerance policy toward sexual exploitation and abuse (SEA) by any staff member, volunteer, contractor, or partner. Anyone found to have violated this policy will face disciplinary action, up to and including termination and referral to law enforcement where appropriate.
safeguarding.reportingBody: If you have witnessed or experienced sexual exploitation, abuse, or any other safeguarding concern, you can report it through our Contact page's dedicated safeguarding channel, or using the phone, WhatsApp, and email details on our Impact & Accountability page. Reports can be made anonymously.
safeguarding.confidentialityBody: All reports are handled confidentially and shared only with those who need to know in order to respond. Making a report, in good faith, will never affect your or your family's access to our assistance, and retaliation against anyone who reports a concern is itself a violation of this policy.
safeguarding.consentBody: We only share a person's story or photograph with their informed consent, and names may be changed to protect identity. We never publish identifiable images of children without an explicit consent record from a parent or guardian.
safeguarding.investigationBody: Every report is reviewed and, where appropriate, investigated by staff responsible for safeguarding. We aim to acknowledge reports promptly and keep the reporter informed of the outcome where safe and appropriate to do so.

terms.heading: Terms of Use
terms.intro: These Terms of Use govern your use of the Horumar Resettlement Network website.
terms.acceptableUseBody: You may use this website to learn about our work, read our news and reports, and contact us. You may not use it to attempt unauthorized access to our systems, to interfere with the site's operation, or for any unlawful purpose.
terms.contentOwnershipBody: Unless otherwise noted, the text, photographs, and other content on this website belong to Horumar Resettlement Network or are used with permission. You may share links to our content, but you may not republish it without our written consent.
terms.noWarrantyBody: This website is provided as-is. While we try to keep information accurate and current, we make no guarantee that it is complete or error-free.
terms.governingLawBody: These terms are governed by the laws of [JURISDICTION TO BE CONFIRMED], pending confirmation of our formal legal registration.
terms.changesBody: We may update these terms from time to time. Continued use of the website after changes are posted means you accept the updated terms.
